speaker:
FIRST
               
              KNIGHT
text: Good morrow to the good Simonides.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Knights, from my daughter this I let you know,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: That for this twelvemonth she’ll not undertake

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: A married life. Her reason to herself is only known,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Which from her by no means can I get.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              KNIGHT
text: May we not get access to her, my lord?

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Faith, by no means; she hath so strictly tied her

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: To her chamber that ’tis impossible.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: One twelve moons more she’ll wear Diana’s livery.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: This by the eye of Cynthia hath she vowed,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And on her virgin honor will not break it.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              KNIGHT
text: Loath to bid farewell, we take our leaves.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: So,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: They are well dispatched. Now to my daughter’s letter.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: She tells me here she’ll wed the stranger knight

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Or never more to view nor day nor light.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: ’Tis well, mistress, your choice agrees with mine.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: I like that well. Nay, how absolute she’s in ’t,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Not minding whether I dislike or no!

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Well, I do commend her choice, and will no longer

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Have it be delayed. Soft, here he comes.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: I must dissemble it.

speaker: PERICLES
text: All fortune to the good Simonides.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: To you as much. Sir, I am beholding to you

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: For your sweet music this last night. I do

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Protest, my ears were never better fed

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: With such delightful pleasing harmony.

speaker: PERICLES
text: It is your Grace’s pleasure to commend,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Not my desert.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Sir, you are music’s master.

speaker: PERICLES
text: The worst of all her scholars, my good lord.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Let me ask you one thing:

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: What do you think of my daughter, sir?

speaker: PERICLES
text: A most virtuous princess.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And she is fair too, is she not?

speaker: PERICLES
text: As a fair day in summer, wondrous fair.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Sir, my daughter thinks very well of you,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Ay, so well that you must be her master,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And she will be your scholar. Therefore, look to it.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I am unworthy for her schoolmaster.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: She thinks not so. Peruse this writing else.

speaker: PERICLES
text: What’s here?

speaker: PERICLES
text: A letter that she loves the knight of Tyre?

speaker: PERICLES
text: ’Tis the King’s subtlety to have my life.—

speaker: PERICLES
text: O, seek not to entrap me, gracious lord,

speaker: PERICLES
text: A stranger and distressèd gentleman

speaker: PERICLES
text: That never aimed so high to love your daughter,

speaker: PERICLES
text: But bent all offices to honor her.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Thou hast bewitched my daughter, and thou art

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: A villain.

speaker: PERICLES
text: By the gods, I have not!

speaker: PERICLES
text: Never did thought of mine levy offense;

speaker: PERICLES
text: Nor never did my actions yet commence

speaker: PERICLES
text: A deed might gain her love or your displeasure.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Traitor, thou liest!

speaker: PERICLES
text: Traitor?

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Ay, traitor.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Even in his throat, unless it be the King

speaker: PERICLES
text: That calls me traitor, I return the lie.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Now, by the gods, I do applaud his courage.

speaker: PERICLES
text: My actions are as noble as my thoughts,

speaker: PERICLES
text: That never relished of a base descent.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I came unto your court for honor’s cause,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And not to be a rebel to her state,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And he that otherwise accounts of me,

speaker: PERICLES
text: This sword shall prove he’s honor’s enemy.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: No?

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Here comes my daughter. She can witness it.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Then as you are as virtuous as fair,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Resolve your angry father if my tongue

speaker: PERICLES
text: Did e’er solicit or my hand subscribe

speaker: PERICLES
text: To any syllable that made love to you.

speaker: THAISA
text: Why, sir, say if you had, who takes offense

speaker: THAISA
text: At that would make me glad?

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Yea, mistress, are you so peremptory?

speaker: SIMONIDES
text:
(
                Aside
                .
                )
                 
              I am glad on ’t with all my heart.—

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: I’ll tame you! I’ll bring you in subjection.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Will you, not having my consent,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Bestow your love and your affections

speaker: SIMONIDES
text:
Upon a stranger?
                 
                (
                Aside
                .
                )
               Who, for aught I know,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: May be—nor can I think the contrary—

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: As great in blood as I myself.—

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Therefore, hear you, mistress: either frame

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Your will to mine—and you, sir, hear you:

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Either be ruled by me—or I’ll make you

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Man and wife.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Nay, come, your hands and lips must seal it too.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And being joined, I’ll thus your hopes destroy.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And for further grief—God give you joy!

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: What, are you both pleased?

speaker: THAISA
text:
Yes,
                 
                (
                to
                 
                Pericles
                )
               if you love me, sir.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Even as my life my blood that fosters it.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: What, are you both agreed?

speaker: BOTH
text: Yes, if ’t please your Majesty.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: It pleaseth me so well that I will see you wed,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And then with what haste you can, get you to bed.
